\begin{table}[htbp]
  \centering
  \caption{Multi-worker deep-fetch throughput (req/s, median of three runs) as each
    layer is scaled to 1, 2, and 4 Express workers (node cluster, shared port). At one
    worker the native driver and Prisma are near parity; as workers consume the
    otherwise-idle cores the single-connection-pool layers scale near-linearly and the
    native driver overtakes Prisma (whose in-process engine already used several
    cores at one worker), so Prisma's parity is specific to a single-worker
    deployment.}
  \label{tab:cluster}
  \begin{tabular}{l r r r}
    \toprule
    Layer & 1~worker & 2~workers & 4~workers \\
    \midrule
    \multicolumn{4}{l}{\emph{PostgreSQL}} \\
    \quad\texttt{pg} & 3299 & 4055 & 3961 \\
    \quad\texttt{prisma} & 1117 & 2275 & 4449 \\
    \quad\texttt{mikroorm} & 435 & 898 & 1773 \\
    \multicolumn{4}{l}{\emph{MySQL}} \\
    \quad\texttt{mysql2} & 2327 & 4698 & 8883 \\
    \quad\texttt{prisma} & 591 & 1167 & 2427 \\
    \quad\texttt{mikroorm} & 418 & 851 & 1624 \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
\end{table}
